primary_actions.reasoning: The RAN tier has the highest contribution (80.6%) to the DDoS prediction, indicating it is the most affected by the attack. Implementing rate limiting here will help manage the traffic load and prevent overwhelming the network.

supporting_actions.reasoning: While the RAN is the dominant party, the Edge tier also contributes significantly to the overall traffic. Traffic scrubbing at this tier will help filter out malicious traffic, complementing the rate limiting action taken in the RAN.

with_rag.overall_reasoning: The RAN tier is prioritized for the primary action due to its dominant contribution to the DDoS attack, while the Edge tier is selected for supporting actions to ensure comprehensive mitigation. This approach leverages the evidence of party contributions effectively.

primary_actions.reasoning: Traffic filtering is essential to mitigate the effects of a DDoS attack by blocking malicious traffic at the core network level.

without_rag.overall_reasoning: Given the 100% confidence in the DDoS prediction, immediate and effective actions are necessary to prevent service disruption.
